meaning: adj.背叛的,背信弃义的,奸诈的,叛逆的
examples: He was weak, cowardly and treacherous.
There is no treacherous blood in her—no, she was always honest and true.
Although a man of blood and violence, Richard was too impetuous to be either treacherous or habitually cruel.
On Monday, many roads across north Wales remained treacherous and some impassable, particularly minor routes.
They can be slippery in the rain - treacherous if you are on a scooter.
